title: FedEx Expands SAF Use to DFW and JFK, Reaching Five U.S. Airports
content: FedEx has expanded its adoption of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) by launching deliveries at Dallas Fort Worth (DFW) and New York-JFK airports in late 2025. Partnering with World Fuel Services, the company secured two million gallons of neat SAF, delivered as a blend, bringing its 2025 total to five U.S. airports and five million gallons of neat cleaner fuel equivalent. At DFW, FedEx became the first carrier to purchase SAF commercially outside of pilot programs. Despite this progress, Chief Sustainability Officer Karen Blanks Ellis highlighted IATA projections of a potential slowdown in SAF production growth for 2026, calling for policies that incentivize supply rather than just regulating demand. Concurrently, FedEx updated its sustainability metrics, now targeting a 40% reduction in aircraft emissions intensity by 2034, having already surpassed its previous 30% reduction goal.
author: Analysts Account
